title: PEHRCIVE - User Documentation
text:
Preface



Figure: PERHCIVE


PEHRCIVE: Platform for Evaluating Human-Robot Collaboration and Interaction in Virtual Environments
This work was developed in the scope of the “TRUSTYCOBOTS: Human-like or machine-like? Tracking psychophysiological components of trust in human-robot collaboration” project.

title: 1  Introduction
text: The following documentation presents the documentation of the PEHRCIVE platform, which stands for “Platform for Evaluating Human-Robot Collaboration and Interaction in Virtual Environments”. This platform is designed to facilitate the evaluation of human-robot collaboration and interaction in virtual environments, providing researchers with tools and resources to conduct experiments and analyze data effectively.

title: 2  Setup
text:
2.1 Hardware Requirements
Before running the software, ensure the hardware is ready:

title: 2  Setup
text:
VR Headset: Connect the HTC Vive or SteamVR headset to your PC.
Sensors: Ensure all base stations are powered and visible.







ImportantHeadset Connection



The application may fail to launch if SteamVR is not detecting the headset. Put on the headset to wake it up before starting the software.

title: 2  Setup
text:
2.2 Software Installation & Launch

Download the latest build of the software from the official PEHRCIVE repository.
Navigate to the software installation folder.
Click PEHRCIVE.exe to launch the application.
Once the application is running, proceed to the Experiment Configuration dashboard.

title: 3  Configuration
text:
3.1 Global Settings
The Experiment Configuration dashboard allows you to design the flow of the experiment. You can create a new configuration or import an existing one.
Located on the left panel, these settings persist throughout the experiment:

title: 3  Configuration
text:
Participant ID: Enter the participant’s ID here.
Spawner Position: Select the human spawner position (Left or Right).
Eye Tracking & Calibration: Enable checkboxes for eye tracking and logging (during the experiment) and eye calibration, prior to the experiment and necessary to ensure accurate data collection.
Data Output Path: Select the output folder for experiment data, the collected data will be saved in a folder with the input from the Participant ID field, inside the folder defined in the Data Output Path.
Load Configuration: Opens a file chooser window that allows users to select a JSON file containing a previously saved configuration.
Save Configuration: Opens a file chooser window that allows users to save the current experimental procedure timeline entries in a JSON file.
Randomization: Randomize the order of the conditions, this only applies to the task conditions (colored green), the remaining experimental blocks order does not change (instructions, questionnaires and pauses).
Load Default Configuration: Loads a default configuration to the experimental procedure timeline to be used as example.
Clear All Configuration: Clears all the entries (experimental blocks) in the experimental procedure timeline.
Start Experiment: Begin the experiment respecting the order and configuration defined in the experimental procedure timeline entries.

title: 3  Configuration
text:
3.2 Experimental Procedure Timeline Settings
You can add specific experimental conditions to the timeline. For each condition, you can configure:

3.2.1 Instructions
Update the instruction text and configure trial (demo) slots.

3.2.1.1 Basic Settings

Name

The name of the instructions experimental block.

Instructions Text

The text displayed inside the virtual environment in the beginning of the instructions scene, to instruct/guide participants.




3.2.1.2 Trial Configuration

Number of Insertion Slots Columns

The number of columns corresponding to the insertion slot grid.

Number of Insertion Slots Rows

The number of rows corresponding to the insertion slot grid.





3.2.2 Task Conditions

Task Parameters: Set the number of trials, squares per trial, and collaboration type (Sequential vs. Simultaneous).
Robot Parameters: Select the robot type (e.g., Kuka) and adjust parameters like Distance, Velocity, and Target Velocity. 
Ambient Sound: Add specific audio files for the condition.



3.2.3 Questionnaires


Questionnaires: Add “Differential Semantic” or “Likert Scale” questionnaires. You can customize the questions and items.



3.2.4 Pauses

Pauses: Set a break timer that can be resumed manually or automatically.

title: 4  Running the Experiment
text:
4.1 VR View & Task Execution
Once the configuration is complete, click Start Experiment.
The participant will see instructions in the virtual display monitor to their right inside the virtual reality environment.

title: 4  Running the Experiment
text:
Initiation: Read the instructions and press the space bar to begin.
The Task: During each trial, grab the colored block and place it in the matching colored area.


4.1.1 Collaboration Modes

Sequential Condition: The user must wait for the robot to complete its turn before acting.
Simultaneous Condition: The user places the block immediately without waiting for the robot.

title: 4  Running the Experiment
text:
4.2 Handling Robot Errors
If you enabled Robot Error Configuration in the settings, the robot may intentionally fail a task, according to the configuration. * Ensure the participant understands to continue the block-grabbing tasks until all trials are complete, regardless of robot behavior.

title: 4  Running the Experiment
text:
4.3 Completion
After finishing all trials, the participant can answer questionnaires using the display monitor to their right inside the virtual reality environment.
